findings.evidence: grep hit: line 60 declares `theorem legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_legendreSym ... : legendreSym q (oddPrimeDiscriminant p) = legendreSym p (q : ℤ)`, while line 79 is `@[simp] theorem legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_legendreSym` if the reciprocal symbol is the intended normal form, or provide an explicitly tagged simp wrapper for `legendreSym q (oddPrimeDiscriminant p)`.
findings.issue: `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_legendreSym` is the characteristic normal form for `(p*/q)`, but it is not tagged `@[simp]`; only the narrower `= 1` wrapper at line 79 is tagged.

summary: The exported Legendre-symbol surface has the right characteristic lemmas, but its main normalization theorem is not registered for automation, so downstream splitting statements cannot simplify the prime-discriminant symbol to the reciprocal symbol without manual rewrites.
